title: Lecture One Weyl-Node No-Go Proof State
summary: Current proof-state ledger for the Lecture One flagship lattice no-go theorem: the local Weyl and Berry-flux branches are explicit, Witten's punctured-zone Stokes proof is explicit, Friedan's cited spectral-projection and cobordism route is now explicit, and the continuum parity-anomaly motivation is now kept separate from the actual lattice proof.
assumptions: The theorem family must preserve the difference between local Weyl physics, the global lattice proof, and later continuum anomaly motivation.
regime: Proof-completion review state for the Lecture One lattice no-go branch.
scope: Ledger-level summary of the Lecture One theorem chain, the cited Friedan route, and the anomaly-versus-lattice scope boundary.

source_anchors.notes: Cited rigorous route now fused locally through explicit proof fragments.

representation: Proof-state ledger for the Lecture One lattice no-go theorem family and its cited-source closure.
pass_conditions: The local Weyl-node charge, punctured-zone Stokes argument, and Berry-curvature reformulation are all explicit and formula-bearing.
The cited Friedan route is explicit through the spectral-projection current and the spectral-bundle Chern-number interpretation.
The proof state explicitly separates continuum parity-anomaly or anomaly-inflow intuition from the actual lattice theorem proof.
The lecture-one family is described as promotion-ready only because the former Friedan and anomaly-separation blockers are now closed locally.
failure_triggers: Fail if the answer cites Friedan only by name without the spectral projection or cobordism formulas.
Fail if the answer replaces the theorem proof with anomaly language.
Fail if the Berry-flux or punctured-zone Stokes branch is erased in favor of only the cited-paper route.
retrieval_hints: Use when an answer or audit needs the full Lecture One theorem ledger rather than one isolated Stokes or Berry-flux step.
